text: The Nuclear Power Options Viability Study (NPOVS) was initiated at the beginning of calendar year 1984 by Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL).
The objective of NPOVS was to explore the viabilities of several nuclear electric power generation options for commercial deployment within the United States beginning in the 2000-2010 time frame.

The NPOVS proceeded in steps: (1) a literature search and development of a bibliography; (2) development of criteria for evaluation of nuclear plant designs and plans; (3) evaluation of selected design concepts using these criteria as a guide; and (4) recommendations for areas of research and development (R&D) needed to reduce uncertainties in the viabilities of options.
The probability of events leading to loss of investment is less than or equal to $10^{-4}$ per year (based on plant costs).

Approximately $30\%$ of the documents are reprints of articles from journals such as Nuclear Engineering International, Power Engineering, Electrical World, Energy Policy, Science and Public Policy, and Technology Review.
Reports and presentations produced by the 31 companies and laboratories in the nuclear field and which were contacted by NPOVS staff comprise another $20\%$ of the collection.
The code consists of two or three letters followed by three sets of two-digit numbers such as NO-03-02-05.
The coding is illustrated below for NO-03-02-05.

\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {N O} = \text {N u c l e a r O p t i o n s} \\ 0 3 = \text {N o t e b o k N o .} 3 (\text {o r F i l e B o x N o .} 3 \text {i f c o d e d ＂ B 0 3 ＂}) \\ 0 2 = \text {S e c t i o n N o .
2 o f t h e n o t e b k} \\ 0 5 = \text {I t e m N o .} 5 \text {i n t h e s e c t i o n}. \\ \end{array}
To facilitate locating citations or documents by subject, a keyword index is provided as Chapter 4.
Please refer to Chapter 4 for details and instructions for utilizing the keyword index.
The bibliographic data base resides in the ORNL IBM 3033 mainframe computer (System 2 on ORNL'S System Select Network) and is accessible to ORNL staff via a search program called ORLOOK.
After log on and initialization of ORLOOK, the user makes the following selections: "private" data base, named "NPOVS," and file number "1."
Combination searches for a maximum of 4 words are also possible, thereby tailoring the search to fit the needs of the user.
For information on other search combinations and detailed information about ORLOOK searches, please refer to ORNL-4951 (Rev.
